revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ca:Fudoki]][[de:Fudoki]][[en:Fudoki]][[es:Fudoki]][[fa:فودوکی]][[fr:Fudoki]][[id:Fudoki]][[it:Fudoki]][[ja:風土記]][[ko:풍토기]][[no:Fudoki]][[pl:Fudoki]][[ru:Фудоки]][[sr:Фудоки]][[uk:Фудокі]][[zh:風土記 (日本)]]
{{for|the novel by Kij Johnson|Fudoki (novel)}}
{{More footnotes|date=November 2011}}
{{Shintoism}} {{Italic title|reason=}}{{Short description|Ancient Japanese reports on local culture and geography}}
{{nihongo|'''''Fudoki'''''|風土記|}} are ancient reports on {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=provincial|1=Provinces of Japan}} culture, geography, and {{ill|Oral tradition|en|Oral tradition|qid=Q209815|lt=Oral tradition|1=Oral tradition}} presented to the reigning {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=monarchs of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}, also known as local [[:en:Gazetteers|Gazetteers]]. They contain agricultural, geographical, and historical records as well as [[Japanese mythology|mythology]] and [[Japanese folklore|folklore]].{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=16-17}} ''Fudoki'' manuscripts also document local {{ill|Myth|en|Myth|qid=Q12827256|lt=Myth|1=Myth}}s, {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|qid=Q189819|lt=Ritual|1=Ritual}}s, and {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|qid=Q737148|lt=poems|1=Waka (poetry)}} that are not mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' {{ill|Chronicle|en|Chronicle|qid=Q185363|lt=Chronicle|1=Chronicle}}s, which are the most important literature of the ancient national mythology and history. In the course of [[Kofun period|national unification]], the {{ill|Imperial Court in Kyoto|en|Imperial Court in Kyoto|qid=Q11680900|lt=imperial court|1=Imperial Court in Kyoto}} enacted a series of criminal and administrative codes called ''[[ritsuryō]]'' and surveyed the provinces established by such codes to exert greater control over them.{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=4-5}}
== Kofudoki == preserved at {{ill|Tenri Central Library|en|Tenri Central Library|qid=Q872328|lt=Tenri Central Library|1=Tenri Central Library}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}]] In the narrower sense, ''Fudoki'' refer to the oldest records written in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, later called {{nihongo|'''''Kofudoki'''''|古風土記}} (Old-Fudoki). Compilation of ''Kofudoki'' began in 713 and was completed over a 20-year period.{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=16-17}} Following the [[Taika Reform]] in 646 and the [[:en:Code of Taihō|Code of Taihō]] enacted in 701, there was need to centralize and solidify the power of the imperial court. This included accounting for lands under its control. According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'', {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}} issued a decree in 713 ordering each {{nihongo|provincial government|[[:ja:国衙]]|kokuga}} to collect and report the following information:{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=16-17}}{{Sfn|Kojima|Naoki|Nishimiya|Kuranaka|2007|pp=310-311}} *Names of {{ill|Districts of Japan|en|Districts of Japan|qid=Q1122846|lt=districts|1=Districts of Japan}} and townships *Natural resources and living things *Land fertility *Etymology of [[Japanese place name#Geographic features|names for geographic features]], such as mountains, plains, and rivers *Myths, legends, and folktales told orally by old people
==Names==
{{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}} ordered in 713 that [[:en:Japanese place names|place names]] in the provinces, districts, and townships be written in two {{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}} characters with positive connotations.<ref>{{cite web | url=http://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000151739 | title= 好字令 (good names) | publisher=National Diet Library | work=Collaborative Reference Database | access-date=28 August 2015}}</ref>{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=16-17}} This occasionally required name changes. For example, {{nihongo|Hayatsuhime|速津媛|}} became {{nihongo|Hayami|速見|}} and {{nihongo|Ishinashi no Oki|石無堡|}} became {{nihongo|Ishii|石井|}}.
==Manuscripts==
At least 48 of the {{ill|Gokishichidō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokishichidō|qq=}} provinces contributed to their records but only that of {{ill|Izumo Province|Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} remains nearly complete. Partial records of {{ill|Hizen Province|Hizen|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, {{ill|Bungo Province|Bungo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, {{ill|Harima Province|Harima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} and {{ill|Hitachi Province|Hitachi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} remain and a few passages from various volumes remain scattered throughout various books.{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|pp=16-17}} Those of Harima and Hizen are designated {{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (writings: Japanese books)#History books and historical tales|National Treasures|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of National Treasures of Japan (writings: Japanese books)#History books and historical tales|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.
Below is a list of extant manuscripts and scattered passages.{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011|p=19}}<ref>[http://homepage2.nifty.com/toka3aki/geography/fudoki_itsu.html 国土としての始原史～風土記逸文] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130104042845/http://homepage2.nifty.com/toka3aki/geography/fudoki_itsu.html |date=2013-01-04 }}</ref>
{{col-begin}} {{col-break}}
===Kinai=== *{{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=}}: ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' Yamashiro Province 風土記 *{{ill|Yamato Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}: ''Yamato no Kuni Fudoki'' Yamato Province 風土記 *{{ill|Settsu Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}: ''Settsu no Kuni Fudoki'' 摂津国風土記
===Tōkaidō=== *{{ill|Iga Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Province|qq=}}: ''Iga no Kuni Fudoki'' 伊賀国風土記 *{{ill|Ise Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}}: ''Ise no Kuni Fudoki'' 伊勢国風土記 *{{ill|Shima Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Province|qq=}}: ''Shima no Kuni Fudoki'' 志摩国風土記 *{{ill|Owari Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}}: ''Owari no Kuni Fudoki'' 尾張国風土記 *{{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}: ''Mikawa no Kuni Fudoki'' 参河(三河)国風土記 *{{ill|Suruga Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Province|qq=}}: ''Suruga no Kuni Fudoki'' {{ill|8=ja|9=駿河国風土記|Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} *{{ill|Izu Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Province|qq=}}: ''Izu no Kuni Fudoki'' 伊豆国風土記 *{{ill|Kai Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Province|qq=}}: ''Kai no Kuni Fudoki'' 甲斐国風土記 *{{ill|Sagami Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}}: ''Sagami no Kuni Fudoki'' 相模国風土記 *{{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}}: ''Shimousa no Kuni Fudoki'' 下総国風土記 *{{ill|Kazusa Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa Province|qq=}}: ''Kazusa no Kuni Fudoki'' 上総国風土記 *{{ill|Hitachi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=}}: '''''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki''''' {{ill|8=ja|9=常陸国風土記|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}
===Tōsandō=== *{{ill|Ōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}: ''Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki'' {{ill|8=ja|9=近江国風土記|Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} *{{ill|Mino Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}: ''Mino no Kuni Fudoki'' 美濃国風土記 *{{ill|Hida Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hida Province|qq=}}: ''Hida no Kuni Fudoki'' 飛騨国風土記 *{{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}: ''Shinano no Kuni Fudoki'' 信濃国風土記 *{{ill|Michinoku Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoku Province|qq=}}: ''Michinoku no Kuni Fudoki'' Mutsu Province 風土記
{{col-break}}
===Hokurikudō=== *{{ill|Wakasa Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Province|qq=}}: ''Wakasa no Kuni Fudoki'' 若狭国風土記 *{{ill|Echizen Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|qq=}}: ''Echizen no Kuni Fudoki'' 越前国風土記 *{{ill|Echigo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=}}: ''Echigo no Kuni Fudoki'' 越後国風土記
===San'indō=== *{{ill|Tango Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province|qq=}}: ''Tango no Kuni Fudoki'' {{ill|8=ja|9=丹後国風土記|Tango no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} *{{ill|Inaba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=}}: ''Inaba no Kuni Fudoki'' Inaba Province 風土記 *{{ill|Hōki Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki Province|qq=}}: ''Hōki no Kuni Fudoki'' 伯耆国風土記 *{{ill|Izumo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}}: '''''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki''''' {{ill|8=ja|9=Izumo Province 風土記|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *{{ill|Iwami Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Province|qq=}}: ''Iwami no Kuni Fudoki'' 石見国風土記
===San'yōdō=== *{{ill|Harima Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}}: '''''Harima no Kuni Fudoki''''' {{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} *{{ill|Mimasaka Province|en|Mimasaka Province|qid=Q936036|lt=Mimasaka Province|1=Mimasaka Province}}: ''Mimasaka no Kuni Fudoki'' 美作国風土記 *{{ill|Bizen Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province|qq=}}: ''Bizen no Kuni Fudoki'' 備前国風土記 *{{ill|Bitchū Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bitchū Province|qq=}}: ''Bitchū no Kuni Fudoki'' 備中国風土記 *{{ill|Bingo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bingo Province|qq=}}: ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki'' {{ill|8=ja|9=備後国風土記|Bingo no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}
{{col-break}}
===Nankaidō=== *{{ill|Kii Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province|qq=}}: ''Kii no Kuni Fudoki'' 紀伊国風土記 *{{ill|Awaji Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaji Province|qq=}}: ''Awaji no Kuni Fudoki'' 淡路国風土記 *{{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province (Tokushima)|qq=}}: ''Awa no Kuni Fudoki'' 阿波国風土記 *{{ill|Sanuki Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}: ''Sanuki no Kuni Fudoki'' 讃岐国風土記 *{{ill|Iyo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Province|qq=}}: ''Iyo no Kuni Fudoki'' 伊予国風土記 *{{ill|Tosa Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Province|qq=}}: ''Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' 土佐国風土記
===Saikaidō=== *{{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province|qq=}}: ''Chikuzen no Kuni Fudoki'' 筑前国風土記 *{{ill|Chikugo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo Province|qq=}}: ''Chikugo no Kuni Fudoki'' 筑後国風土記 *{{ill|Buzen Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Province|qq=}}: ''Buzen no Kuni Fudoki'' 豊前国風土記 *{{ill|Bungo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}: '''''Bungo no Kuni Fudoki''''' {{ill|8=ja|9=豊後国風土記|Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} *{{ill|Hizen Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Province|qq=}}: '''''Hizen no Kuni Fudoki''''' {{ill|8=ja|9=肥前国風土記|Hizen no Kuni Fudoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} *{{ill|Higo Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Province|qq=}}: ''Higo no Kuni Fudoki'' 肥後国風土記 *{{ill|Hyūga Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyūga Province|qq=}}: ''Hyūga no Kuni Fudoki'' 日向国風土記 *{{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsumi Province|qq=}}: ''Ōsumi no Kuni Fudoki'' 大隅国風土記 *{{ill|Satsuma Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=}}: ''Satsuma no Kuni Fudoki'' Satsuma Province 風土記 *{{ill|Iki Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Province|qq=}}: ''Iki no Kuni Fudoki'' 壱岐国風土記
{{col-end}}
==Parks== In 1966 the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs (Japan)|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] called on the prefectural governments to build [[:en:Open-air museums|Open-air museums]] and parks called {{Nihongo|Fudoki no Oka|風土記の丘|"Fudoki Hills"|qq=draft}} near historic sites such as tombs (''[[kofun]]'') and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}}s.{{Sfn|Sakamoto|2011}} These archaeological museums preserve and exhibit cultural properties to enhance public understanding of provincial history and culture.
{{Gallery |title= |width= |lines= |File:Gyoda_Futagoyama_Kofun_In_Spring_1.jpg|Futagoyama ''kofun'' in Sakitama Fudoki no Oka |File:Azuchi.jpg|{{ill|Azuchi Castle|en|Azuchi Castle|qid=Q786888|lt=Azuchi Castle|1=Azuchi Castle}} ruin in Ōmi Fudoki no Oka |File:Pit House in Kiifudoki-no-oka museum of history.jpg|[[:en:Pit house|Pit house]] at the Kiifudoki-no-oka Museum of History }}
{| class="wikitable" |- ! Name !! Prefecture !! Province !! Municipalities !! Museum |- | Ukitamu Fudoki no Oka || {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} || {{ill|Uzen Province|en|Uzen Province|qid=Q1192050|lt=Uzen|1=Uzen Province}} || {{ill|Takahata, Yamagata|en|Takahata, Yamagata|qid=Q772103|lt=Takahata|1=Takahata, Yamagata}} || Yamagata Prefectural Ukitama Fudoki no Oka Archaeological Museum |- | Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka || {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} || {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke Province}} || {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qid=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}} || Tochigi Prefectural Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum |- | Nasu Fudoki no Oka || Tochigi || Shimotsuke ||{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|qid=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} and [[:en:Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] || Nakagawa Municipal Nasu Fudoki no Oka Museum |- | {{ill|Sakita Kofun Park|ja|3=さきたま古墳公園|lt=Sakitama Fudoki no Oka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakita Kofun Park|1=Sakitama Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17224997}} || {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama|1=Saitama Prefecture}} || {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi|1=Musashi Province}} || [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]] || [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns]]
|- | {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura|ja|3=千葉県立房総のむら|lt=Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Boso-no-Mura|1=Bōsō Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406280}} || {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba|1=Chiba Prefecture}} || {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa|1=Shimōsa Province}}|| {{ill|Sakae, Chiba|en|Sakae, Chiba|qid=Q1202754|lt=Sakae|1=Sakae, Chiba}} and {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|qid=Q273798|lt=Narita|1=Narita, Chiba}} || Boso-no-Mura Museum |- | Tateyama Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama|1=Toyama Prefecture}} || {{ill|Etchū Province|en|Etchū Province|qid=Q906899|lt=Etchū|1=Etchū Province}} ||{{ill|Tateyama, Toyama|en|Tateyama, Toyama|qid=Q670189|lt=Tateyama|1=Tateyama, Toyama}}||{{ill|Toyama Prefectural Tateyama Museum|ja|3=富山県立山博物館|lt=Toyama Prefectural Tateyama Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefectural Tateyama Museum|1=Tateyama Museum of Toyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11456516}} |- | {{ill|Kai Fudoki no Oka|ja|3=甲斐風土記の丘|lt=Kai Fudoki no Oka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Fudoki no Oka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園|1=Kai Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11515320}}||{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} || {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|qid=Q858076|lt=Kai|1=Kai Province}} ||{{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kōfu|1=Kōfu}}||Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum |- | Ōmi Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga|1=Shiga Prefecture}} || {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi|1=Ōmi Province}} ||[[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]] and {{ill|Azuchi, Shiga|en|Azuchi, Shiga|qid=Q165676|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi, Shiga}} ||{{ill|Shiga Prefectural Azuchi Castle Archaeological Museum|ja|3=滋賀県立安土城考古博物館|lt=Shiga Prefectural Azuchi Castle Archaeological Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefectural Azuchi Castle Archaeological Museum|1=Shiga Prefectural Azuchi Castle Archaeological Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564645}} |- | Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}} || {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi|1=Kawachi Province}}||{{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan|1=Kanan, Osaka}}||{{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qid=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} |- | Kii Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} || {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii|1=Kii Province}}||{{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}||{{ill|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|en|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|qid=Q11417470|lt=Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|1=Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore}} |- | Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane|1=Shimane Prefecture}} || {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}}||{{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}||Shimane Prefectural Yakumotatsu Fudoki no Oka Museum |- | Kibiji Fudoki no Oka ||{{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama Prefecture}} || {{ill|Bitchū Province|en|Bitchū Province|qid=Q1047514|lt=Bitchū|1=Bitchū Province}} ||{{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|qid=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}||Sōja Kibiji Museum |- | {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka|ja|3=広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka|1=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11484763}}||{{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} || {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo|1=Bingo Province}} ||{{ill|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|en|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|qid=Q840591|lt=Miyoshi|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}||Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Museum |- | Higo Kodai no Mori ||{{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} || {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo|1=Higo Province}} ||{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}} and {{ill|Nagomi, Kumamoto|en|Nagomi, Kumamoto|qid=Q1156415|lt=Nagomi|1=Nagomi, Kumamoto}}||[[Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Kofun Museum]] |- | {{ill|Usa Fudoki no Oka|ja|3=宇佐風土記の丘|lt=Usa Fudoki no Oka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Fudoki no Oka|1=Usa Fudoki Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449110}}||{{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita|1=Ōita Prefecture}} || {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo|1=Bungo Province}}||{{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|qid=Q114147|lt=Usa|1=Usa, Ōita}}||{{ill|Ōita Prefectural Museum of History|en|Ōita Prefectural Museum ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071020104321/http://applepig.idv.tw/kuon/furu/text/fuudo/fundo_top.htm 風土記](Big5 Chinese) texts of the remaining Fudoki & scattered passages in other books. *Manuscript scans at {{ill|Waseda University Library|en|Waseda University Library|qid=Q7971387|lt=Waseda University Library|1=Waseda University Library}}: [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_04794/ru04_04794.html Hizen, 1800],[http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_04764/ru04_04764.html Bungo, 1800], [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_01062/ru04_01062.html Bungo, unknown] * {{cite book|last=Tsukamoto|first=Tetsuzō ({{lang|ja|塚本 哲三}})|title=Kojiki, Norito, Fudoki ({{lang|ja-Hant|古事記・祝詞・風土記}})|pages=383–586|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten ({{lang|ja|有朋堂書店}})|year=1915|id={{Internet Archive|kojikits00tsukuoft|Scan}}}} *[http://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/fuudo/fundo_top.htm 風土記] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://archive.today/20130104042845/http://homepage2.nifty.com/toka3aki/geography/fudoki_itsu.html 国土としての始原史～風土記逸文] {{in lang|ja}}
